title_en: 2. Striking Cheeks and Tearing of Clothes
content_en: It was the practice in Arabia at the time of the Prophet (r) for women in mourning to slap or scratch the cheeks of their faces and to rip their clothes. These acts were strictly forbidden as well as all similar practices which involved self punishment and the destruction of material wealth. These practices all aim at heightening the pain and sorrow accompanying death while Islaam seeks to minimize it. Ibn MasвҖҳood reported that the Prophet (r) said, )) Щҗпә”о ЁпәҺп»«Щҗп» Щҗп»ҙо  п»Ҝ пәҚп»ҹп№ҫпә о  п»ӯо ўп»Ӣо  пәҺ пә’Щҗпә©о  п»Ӣо   пә©о ў пәғЩҺп»ӯо  п»ӯпәҸо Ўп»ҙо Ў пәёп№¶п»•пұ  пәҚп»ҹп№ҫпә о ў пәғЩҺп»ӯо  п»ӯпә©о Ў пәҚп»ҹп№ҫпәЁп№ёпә©о  пәҸо  пәӯо   п»Җо ўп»Ҙо   п»ӨЩҗп»Ёпұ пәҺ п»Өо  пәұо ў п»ҹп№¶п»ҙ ((. вҖңWhoever slaps his face, tears his clothes or invites (others) to an un-Islamic slogan is not of us.вҖқ40
content_id: Kebiasaan di Arabia pada zaman Nabi п·ә adalah para wanita yang berkabung menampar atau menggaruk pipi mereka dan merobek pakaian mereka. Tindakan-tindakan ini dilarang keras, demikian pula semua praktik serupa yang melibatkan penyiksaan diri dan perusakan harta benda. Semua praktik ini bertujuan memperparah rasa sakit dan duka yang menyertai kematian, sementara Islam berupaya meminimalkannya. Ibnu Mas'ud melaporkan bahwa Nabi п·ә bersabda, "Bukan termasuk golongan kami orang yang menampar wajahnya, merobek pakaiannya, atau menyerukan seruan kebodohan (jahiliah)."
